key_insight: A general-purpose atomic cluster expansion (ACE) potential can model niobium with near-DFT accuracy across diverse crystal defects, phonons, high-pressure states, and fracture scenarios.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, large-scale atomistic simulation of niobium for structural reliability and extreme-environment design without relying on full DFT calculations.
method: Trained an ACE interatomic potential on thousands of DFT structures covering many local atomic environments.
Validated the model against phonons, high-pressure behavior, dislocation glide barriers, and fracture.
Compared performance with empirical and machine-learning potentials, including a new universal ML model.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports training on thousands of DFT configurations and testing the ACE in a near-million-atom molecular dynamics fracture simulation.
watch_item: Despite strong transferability, the potential still requires careful validation outside the niobium environments represented in the training set.
paper_title: A general-purpose atomic cluster expansion interatomic potential for niobium
